title: sedum altissimum subsp. nicaeense
intro: Sedum altissimum subsp. nicaeense, also known as a subspecies of the tall white sedum, is a type of succulent plant that belongs to the family Crassulaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of sedum altissimum subsp. nicaeense include reducing inflammation and improving wound healing, as well as exhibiting potential antioxidant and antiviral activities. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made by infusing the leaves and stems in hot water to create a tea or by using the plant's extracts in topical creams and ointments.
